channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Apple Tries Again On AI, Turns To Google For Help

media:content.media:description: Apple's more deliberate pace in entering the AI frenzy has won praise.

item.description: Tim Cook two years ago announced that Apple was making a major leap to embrace AI, in a program called Apple Intelligence.
item.content:encoded: Tim Cook two years ago announced that Apple was making a major leap to embrace AI, in a program called Apple Intelligence.

item.title: A War Trump, Netanyahu Started Together Is Now Driving Them Apart

media:content.media:description: Donald Trump has been publicly at odds with Netanyahu over the military operation.

item.description: Iran wants Lebanon included in any wider regional truce, a demand Trump seems to have accepted in order to get a deal.
item.content:encoded: Iran wants Lebanon included in any wider regional truce, a demand Trump seems to have accepted in order to get a deal.

item.title: Trump Gets Cold Hometown Welcome At NBA Finals In New York

media:content.media:description: Trump, a long-time Knicks fan and native New Yorker, last visited Madison Square Garden in November 2024.

item.description: US President Donald Trump was booed ahead of the NBA Finals matchup at Madison Square Garden on Monday, as his attendance prompted ultra-tight security at...
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump was booed ahead of the NBA Finals matchup at Madison Square Garden on Monday, as his attendance prompted ultra-tight security at...

item.title: Charlie Kirk's Death Prompted Usha Vance To Try For 4th Child, Says JD Vance

media:content.media:description: The Vances already have three children-- Ewan (8), Vivek (6) and Mirabel (4).

item.description: According to the US leader, the conversation with Erika Kirk changed "something" for Usha Vance, and "not long after we buried my friend, she became pregnant with our fourth child, a boy."
item.content:encoded: According to the US leader, the conversation with Erika Kirk changed "something" for Usha Vance, and "not long after we buried my friend, she became pregnant with our fourth child, a boy."

item.title: US School Shooting Survivor Sues AI Gun Detection Company

media:content.media:description: Nashville teen files suit against AI gun detection maker for failing during school shooting.

item.description: Lawsuit claims AI detection system had operational flaws that missed handgun in Nashville school attack.
item.content:encoded: Lawsuit claims AI detection system had operational flaws that missed handgun in Nashville school attack.

item.title: Airlines Spent 78% More On Jet Fuel Despite Lesser Utilisation: US Data

media:content.media:description: Airlines have raised airfares and fees, cut other perks and canceled flights or trimmed schedules.

item.description: US carriers spent $6.5 billion on fuel in April, compared with about $3.6 billion a year earlier, according to the Bureau of Transportation Statistics
item.content:encoded: US carriers spent $6.5 billion on fuel in April, compared with about $3.6 billion a year earlier, according to the Bureau of Transportation Statistics

item.title: Outdoor Hospitals, Cut-off Communities As Philippine Earthquake Kills 41

media:content.media:description: A series of powerful aftershocks rocked the area from about two hours after the first quake

item.description: In the hardest-hit Sarangani province, some areas remain accessible only by helicopter, and fears of aftershocks were slowing rescue efforts, local officials told reporters at a Tuesday briefing.
item.content:encoded: In the hardest-hit Sarangani province, some areas remain accessible only by helicopter, and fears of aftershocks were slowing rescue efforts, local officials told reporters at a Tuesday briefing.

item.title: Prince William To Evict Rent Free Royals In Major Shake Up When He Becomes King: Report

media:content.media:description: Prince of Wales signals royal property rules will change when he becomes king.

item.description: A source said William is "mindful of how much the monarchy costs" and will be "hands on" in restructuring to cut costs.
item.content:encoded: A source said William is "mindful of how much the monarchy costs" and will be "hands on" in restructuring to cut costs.

item.title: US Army's 1st Apache Gunship Crashes Near Hormuz, Both Pilots Rescued
item.description: The US military has used Apaches, along with MQ-9 Reaper drones and F/A-18 and F-35 fighter jets, as part of Central Command's operation challenging Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz to most commercial traffic.
item.content:encoded: The US military has used Apaches, along with MQ-9 Reaper drones and F/A-18 and F-35 fighter jets, as part of Central Command's operation challenging Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz to most commercial traffic.

item.title: How An Ex-CIA Officer Allegedly Used A Secret Loophole To Collect $40 Million In Gold

media:content.media:description: David Rush was arrested after the FBI raided his Auburn, Virginia, home May 18.

item.description: A former CIA officer has been accused of exploiting a classified intelligence loophole to allegedly obtain $40 million in gold bars. The case has sparked concerns about oversight, secrecy and accountability within America's intelligence community.
item.content:encoded: A former CIA officer has been accused of exploiting a classified intelligence loophole to allegedly obtain $40 million in gold bars. The case has sparked concerns about oversight, secrecy and accountability within America's intelligence community.

item.title: Pentagon Labels Alibaba, Baidu, BYD As Aiding Chinese Military

media:content.media:description: In naming Alibaba, the Pentagon said the tech giant helps boost China's defense industrial base.

item.description: This year's list has grown to 188 Chinese entities, up from last year's roughly 130 named by the Pentagon.
item.content:encoded: This year's list has grown to 188 Chinese entities, up from last year's roughly 130 named by the Pentagon.

item.title: US To Offer Expedited Visa Interviews At Select Embassies For $750

media:content.media:description: The pilot program will run through the end of the year but could be extended depending on demand.

item.description: The move is a potential effort to ease conditions caused by the Trump administration's push to make entering the United States more difficult.
item.content:encoded: The move is a potential effort to ease conditions caused by the Trump administration's push to make entering the United States more difficult.

item.title: US Iran War Live: Crew Rescued After US Helicopter Goes Down Near Hormuz

media:content.media:description: West Asia War, US-Iran-Israel War Live Updates

item.description: US Iran War LIVE Updates: Iran closed the airspace around Tehran's Imam Khomeini International Airport, the country's main airfield, after the Israeli attack.
item.content:encoded: US Iran War LIVE Updates: Iran closed the airspace around Tehran's Imam Khomeini International Airport, the country's main airfield, after the Israeli attack.

item.title: Killing The Mood: Smartphones Reduce Birth Rate, Say Studies

media:content.media:description: "Is the iPhone Birth Control?" asked a paper published by the National Bureau of Economic Research.

item.description: As governments around the world struggle with ways to reverse plunging birth rates, new US studies suggest they have ignored a...
item.content:encoded: As governments around the world struggle with ways to reverse plunging birth rates, new US studies suggest they have ignored a...

item.title: Video: Phone Slips Near 262-Foot Waterfall, Tourist Takes Risky Plunge To Recover It

media:content.media:description: Video shows the man getting close to the deadly drop.

item.description: The man was not identified, and no serious injuries were reported, but the incident drew reactions online.
item.content:encoded: The man was not identified, and no serious injuries were reported, but the incident drew reactions online.

item.title: "In Spirit Of Friendship": Israel To Install Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's Statue In A 'Big' City

media:content.media:description: Fadnavis has vowed to extend his full support for the initiative.

item.description: Revach said that to make the memorial worthy of Shivaji's legacy, Israel has sought cooperation from Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis regarding historical references, artistic consultation, and design considerations, and "he immediately a
item.content:encoded: Revach said that to make the memorial worthy of Shivaji's legacy, Israel has sought cooperation from Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis regarding historical references, artistic consultation, and design considerations, and "he immediately a

item.title: United Nations Warns Of 'Deepening Crisis' In Oceans, Urges Action

media:content.media:description: Oceans play a critical role for the planet, regulating the climate and feeding billions of people.

item.description: Oceans are in a "deepening crisis" that demands urgent global action, a major UN report warned Monday, with seas warming and rising faster, ice cover shrinking...
item.content:encoded: Oceans are in a "deepening crisis" that demands urgent global action, a major UN report warned Monday, with seas warming and rising faster, ice cover shrinking...

item.title: "Lovers' Spat": Israeli Envoy On Trump's Iran Ultimatum To Netanyahu

media:content.media:description: Netanyahu said he told Trump that "Israel has a full right to self-defence".

item.description: Trump, who has reportedly grown increasingly exasperated with Netanyahu, had earlier urged both Israel and Iran to stop "shooting" and said that "final negotiations" towards peace would proceed "subject to ignorance or stupidity getting in its way"
item.content:encoded: Trump, who has reportedly grown increasingly exasperated with Netanyahu, had earlier urged both Israel and Iran to stop "shooting" and said that "final negotiations" towards peace would proceed "subject to ignorance or stupidity getting in its way"

item.title: 'Bibi, You Will Be On Your Own': Trump Warns Netanyahu Over Renewed Iran War
item.description: US President Donald Trump told Axios in an interview published on Monday that he warned Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu that he might find himself fighting alone if he went back to war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump told Axios in an interview published on Monday that he warned Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu that he might find himself fighting alone if he went back to war with Iran.

item.title: 4 Killed, 10 Injured In Russian Strikes On Ukraine's Kharkiv Region

media:content.media:description: At least 15,850 civilians have been killed in Ukraine since Russia launched its invasion in 2022.

item.description: Russian strikes killed four people and wounded 10 others in Ukraine's northeastern Kharkiv region, its governor said early Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: Russian strikes killed four people and wounded 10 others in Ukraine's northeastern Kharkiv region, its governor said early Tuesday.
